title: Analysis of Singapore Banks
subtitle: DBS · OCBC · UOB — income engines, deposits, NIM, valuation (FY2016–2026)
summary: A reconciled, source-graded view of Singapore's three major banks as a regional wealth hub.
conclusions_status: in report.md (Conclusions section, between conclusions markers); answers to the 6 reframed key questions — Q1/Q3/Q4/Q5/Q6 answered (Q5/Q6 from the new Table 6 peer benchmark indices, HSBC = 100), Q2 pending fetch-flows, thesis score 65/100 (down from 70: monetization in line with index bank, not superior; DBS/OCBC premia demand +9-12%/yr outperformance on capital-base and revenue lenses); Q5/Q6 updated 2026-07-24 closed-book by Claude (CwClFable5) — non-Claude cross-model rerun still advisable

pipeline.refresh_note: 2026.07.24-r2: Q5/Q6 answered from peer data - fetch-peers ran 2026-07-24 (Perplexity harness, PxClOpus4.8, PR #26; SG cross-check vs ledger 10/12 exact); new Table 6 in Key Data (monetization + valuation indices vs HSBC=100, lifted from build_benchmarks.py output); Conclusions Q5 [±] in-line-with-index-bank, Q6 [-] DBS/OCBC premia demand +9-12%/yr for 5 years, UOB near parity; thesis score 70 -> 65; Q2 (flows) remains the only pending question.
